title: C# Comments
content:
### Comments in C#

Comments are non-executable text used to document your code. C# supports three types of comments.

### Single-Line Comments

Start with `//` and extend to the end of the line:

```csharp
// This is a single-line comment
int x = 5; // Inline comment
```

### Multi-Line Comments

Enclosed between `/*` and `*/`:

```csharp
/*
  This is a multi-line comment.
  It can span multiple lines.
*/
int y = 10;
```

### XML Documentation Comments

Start with `///` and produce API documentation:

```csharp
/// <summary>
/// Calculates the area of a circle.
/// </summary>
/// <param name="radius">The radius of the circle.</param>
/// <returns>The area of the circle.</returns>
double CalculateArea(double radius)
{
    return Math.PI * radius * radius;
}
```

| Comment Type | Syntax | Use Case |
|-------------|--------|----------|
| Single-line | `// text` | Short explanations |
| Multi-line | `/* text */` | Longer descriptions |
| XML Doc | `/// <tag>` | API documentation |

> **Key Point:** XML documentation comments (///) can generate documentation files and are used by IDEs to provide IntelliSense tooltips. Use them for public APIs.

Comments should explain WHY, not WHAT. The code itself shows what it does.

quiz.question: Which symbol starts a single-line comment in C#?
What is the purpose of XML documentation comments (///)?
